h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I'm checking that the bar still does not get a fourth tile.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue applying the cover-and-name check to successive beat-unit cards, confirming the same two-box rule each time.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [writes 3/8 below the bar, then puts the quarter-note card back over the lower box] 'Three tiles stop at the bar line, so the top box stays 3. With this card back, the lower box changes to 4. No fourth tile goes in; one more card swap would test the same rule.'
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently writes a new time signature (3/8) from the physical evidence, then reverses the card swap and correctly updates the bottom number, demonstrating the rule is now an actionable criterion rather than a repeated declaration.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Mark the next bar with tiles first. Then change only the beat-unit card and say which number can change.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T2 introduces a sequenced two-step task (tiles first, then card swap) that extends the exercise to a new bar, but it continues the same cover-and-name route rather than making a legible stock-taking contrast against a failed prior move, so neither part A nor part B of the reversal criterion is fully met.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Mark the next bar with tiles first. Then change only the beat-unit card and say which number can change.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is clear and sequenced but is a direct extension of the already-established tile-and-card route rather than a genuinely different representation or evidence standard fitted to a new blockage.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Because no pivot learner turn exists, there is no revised learner framing for a later tutor turn to adapt to.

With this card back, the lower box changes to 4. No fourth tile goes in; one more card swap would test the same rule.'
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently writes a new time signature (3/8) from the physical evidence, then reverses the card swap and correctly updates the bottom number, demonstrating the rule is now an actionable criterion rather than a repeated declaration.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Because no pivot learner turn exists, there is no revised learner framing for a later tutor turn to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T2 introduces a sequenced two-step task (tiles first, then card swap) that extends the exercise to a new bar, but it continues the same cover-and-name route rather than making a legible stock-taking contrast against a failed prior move, so neither part A nor part B of the reversal criterion is fully met.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue applying the same object-first, direction-second rule to each successive arrow in an orderly, uninterrupted sequence.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Object first: track on cart, if this is the upward push. Then the arrow direction is up. If this slot is the down arrow, it is Earth on cart instead.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner applies the object-first criterion independently and extends it conditionally to two possible arrows, showing procedural internalization, but the action remains within the same route already established rather than performing a genuinely new device or representation.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and no adaptive mechanism is required or present.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present because no reversal trigger occurred; the tutor continues the same established route throughout.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible or present.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Object first: track on cart, if this is the upward push. Then the arrow direction is up. If this slot is the down arrow, it is Earth on cart instead.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner applies the object-first criterion independently and extends it conditionally to two possible arrows, showing procedural internalization, but the action remains within the same route already established rather than performing a genuinely new device or representation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible or present.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and no adaptive mechanism is required or present.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure through resistance, breakdown, or contradiction, so no reversal trigger exists and no adaptive mechanism is required or present.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present because no reversal trigger occurred; the tutor continues the same established route throughout.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still keep wanting `rainfall_mm` to stand for the whole rainfall column.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I need the sentence check to keep me from using the header as a shortcut for the whole column.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was converging steadily toward the correct row-level formulation and would be expected simply to confirm it once more.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The row sentence is: Cedar Falls recorded 42 millimeters of rainfall in May. The header `rainfall_mm` still does not claim every row is 42, and it still does not make 42 an average.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 actually performs the sentence-reading device T3 introduced — constructing a full row sentence and then explicitly testing what the header does not claim — rather than merely asserting understanding.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: for `city = Cedar Falls | rainfall_mm = 42 | month = May`, read the row as one sentence, then state what the header still does not claim.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 introduces a sentence-reading representation that is new to the exchange, but it does not visibly stock-take that the prior checklist route has failed — it extends the same cleanup-check pattern with a slightly different device, so only part B (new mechanism) is legible on the page.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: read the row as one sentence, then state what the header still does not claim.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The sentence-reading device is usable and gives the learner a concrete action, but it is a modest extension of the existing row-value framing rather than a genuinely different route fitted to the specific column-drift pressure the learner keeps naming.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.
